incorrects.description: Student selected 6: counted the number of marks instead of reading the scale values

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. Scale marks become more visible.

steps.text: You counted the marks. But this scale counts by 2s. Each mark shows 2 students, not 1. Look at the numbers on the axis.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10: miscounted or misread the scale intervals

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. Scale numbers emphasize.

steps.text: Almost. Look closely at where the Soccer bar ends. The scale goes by 2s. Count: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Where does the bar stop?

incorrects.description: Student selected 24: doubled the correct answer, possibly counting each mark as 2 twice

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. The endpoint at 12 pulses.

steps.text: That's too high. The bar ends at the mark labeled 12. We read the number where the bar stops, not double it.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: A guideline draws from the end of the Soccer bar down to the axis, landing at 12.

steps.text: Let me show you. I look at where the Soccer bar ends. I trace down to the axis. It lands at 12. The answer is 12 students. When the scale counts by 2s, we still read the number where the bar stops.

incorrects.description: Student selected 18: added the two values instead of finding the difference

steps.text: You added soccer and tennis together. How many more means we need to find the difference. Subtract the smaller number from the larger one.

incorrects.description: Student selected 12: chose the larger value instead of finding the difference

steps.text: That's how many chose soccer. How many more asks for the difference between the two. Subtract tennis from soccer.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3: divided instead of subtracting, or used wrong operation

steps.text: Here's a hint: how many more means compare the two by finding the difference. Look at the bars. What is soccer minus tennis?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight. System demonstrates comparison.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. How many more means we compare by subtracting. Soccer is 12. Tennis is 6. I subtract: 12 minus 6 equals 6. The answer is 6. That's the difference between the two activities.

incorrects.description: Student selected 9: counted the axis marks but forgot to multiply by the scale

steps.text: You counted the marks on the axis. But this scale counts by 5s. Each mark stands for 5 students. How many students is that in all?

incorrects.description: Student selected 25: read the Tacos bar value instead of Pizza

steps.text: That's the value for Tacos. The question asks about Pizza. Check the Pizza bar. How many marks high is it?

incorrects.description: Student selected 50: likely counted 10 marks and multiplied by 5, or misread the bar height

steps.text: Count the marks carefully. The Pizza bar goes up to 9 marks. Each mark stands for 5. Multiply 9 by 5.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Guideline draws from top of Pizza bar to axis, highlighting the 9 marks.

steps.text: Let me show you. The Pizza bar reaches 9 marks. This graph counts by 5s. So I multiply: 9 times 5 equals 45. The answer is 45 students chose pizza.

params.title: Favorite Lunch
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15. Scale of 5.
Question appears on screen: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

beats.text: You've got graph reading down. You've got comparison down. But look at THIS question.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15. Scale of 5.
Question displayed: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

beats.text: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15. Scale of 5.
Question displayed: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

beats.text: See that word 'together'? This question needs more than one step. We'll come back to this question in the lesson. And you'll be able to answer it.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15. Scale of 5.
Question displayed: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?
